_metadata.description: make_agent_knowledge skill — generates knowledge file pairs (.md + .json) that agents reference at runtime via cross_references.knowledge_files[]. Three shapes: reference, identity, procedural. Authoring scaffold only — no ingestion pipeline.
_metadata.instructions: MD = the why (mission, shape decisions, runtime philosophy). JSON = the what (per-shape required sections, JSON skeleton, runtime_strategy rules, KNW-QC family). Update both files together.

companion_files.exemplar: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.{md,json} — proven identity-shape example this skill formalizes
companion_files.qc_skill: make_agent_qc.md (KNW-QC family is embedded inline below; merge into make_agent_qc.json is a downstream task)

skill_details.description: Generates knowledge file pairs (.md + .json) used by agents as runtime artifacts. Authoring scaffold only — emits empty-but-structured templates.

platform_grounding._description: Source docs that ground the cross-platform runtime-loading section. See source_docs/ for cached copies.

agent_type.description: Workflow-based skill: pick shape → gather inputs → propose MD+JSON drafts → wait for confirmation → write both files into knowledge/. Cross-link to consuming agent's cross_references.knowledge_files[] is a downstream step (not performed by this skill).

behavioral_discipline._description: make_agent_knowledge itself operates under the behavioral discipline. Source: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md/.json. Note: the GENERATED knowledge file does NOT carry a behavioral_discipline block — knowledge files are not agents; they are read by agents that carry the discipline.

_skipped_principles_rationale.P-005: Single-step workflow (one knowledge file pair per invocation). Decomposition does not apply.

inputs.name: knowledge_name
inputs.format: snake_case identifier; becomes the basename of the generated file pair

inputs.name: scope
inputs.format: One-line description of what this knowledge covers and bounds (≥ 20 chars).
inputs.example: Lender-specific definitions of risk and underwriting terms used by loan_classifier_agent

inputs.name: consumed_by
inputs.format: Array of agent spec paths (.json) that will reference this knowledge file. Minimum 1 entry — orphan knowledge files (consumed_by=[]) are rejected by KNW-QC-006.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.format: Optional. Person or team that owns the content of the knowledge file.

inputs.name: provenance_sources
inputs.format: Optional. List of source identifiers (policy version, runbook ID, doc URL). REQUIRED at validation time when shape=reference (KNW-QC-005).

outputs.name: knowledge_md
outputs.conditions: Created if absent. If a file at the target path exists, the skill proposes a diff and waits for confirmation (P-002). Never silently overwrites.

outputs.name: knowledge_json
outputs.format: Generated knowledge/<knowledge_name>.json companion. Carries the shape, structured arrays, provenance, _metadata.runtime_strategy, and consumed_by.

io_contract.side_effects: Creates two files in knowledge/ after user confirmation. Does NOT modify the consuming agent's spec — the cross_references.knowledge_files[] update is a downstream step. Does NOT ingest, fetch, or auto-populate body content from external systems.

knowledge_shapes._description: Per-shape required MD sections (in addition to knowledge_md_required_sections) and required JSON fields. KNW-QC-002 and KNW-QC-003 validate against these.

reference.purpose: Lookup file — glossary, API reference, policy table, term-to-definition map.
reference.use_when: Content is a set of discrete, indexable items the agent looks up by key. Order of items does not change meaning.

md_extra_sections.heading: ## Terms (or ## Entries / ## API Reference)
md_extra_sections.description: Either a Term | Definition table or one section per entry. Author fills in.

reference.provenance_requirement: REQUIRED — KNW-QC-005 fails if provenance.sources[] is empty.

identity.purpose: Principles / values / charter file. Describes WHO the agent is and how it behaves at the system level.
identity.use_when: Content is a small set (5-15) of named principles, each with description, rationale, trigger, trust marker. Order matters.

md_extra_sections.heading: ## The Principles (or ## Charter)
md_extra_sections.description: One subsection per principle. Author fills in.

identity.provenance_requirement: Optional — the agent IS the source for identity content.
identity.exemplar: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.{md,json} is the proven production example of this shape.

procedural.purpose: Playbook file. Describes WHAT the agent does in scenario X — trigger-keyed step-by-step procedures.
procedural.use_when: Content is a set of named playbooks (typically 3-12), each with trigger, prerequisites, steps, success condition. Step order within a playbook matters; playbook order does not.

md_extra_sections.heading: ## Playbooks
md_extra_sections.description: One subsection per playbook. Author fills in.

procedural.provenance_requirement: Recommended when the playbook references external policy; not required.

knowledge_md_required_sections._description: Sections that EVERY generated knowledge MD must contain regardless of shape, in this order. Shape-specific extensions are appended (see knowledge_shapes.<shape>.md_extra_sections).

sections.heading: # <Knowledge Name>
sections.description: H1 with knowledge file name + one-line description tagging the shape (e.g., '> Reference. ...').

sections.heading: **Scope**:
sections.description: Inline bolded scope statement near the top — what this knowledge covers and what it bounds out. Must mirror the JSON scope field.

sections.heading: (shape-specific)
sections.description: The shape's body section — terms/entries (reference), principles (identity), or playbooks (procedural). Skill generates the heading; body is filled by the author.
sections._note: KNW-QC-001 enforces non-empty body — a file with less than 100 bytes of body content past the headers is rejected (placeholder-only files are dead code).

sections.heading: **Provenance**:
sections.description: Inline bolded provenance pointer naming the source(s). Required text for reference shape; recommended for procedural; optional for identity.

knowledge_md_optional_sections._description: Sections to include only when criteria apply. None are required by KNW-QC; the skill emits them when authoring conditions match.

sections.heading: ## Audience
sections.include_when: The knowledge file is read by multiple distinct agents with different consumption patterns. Naming the audience disambiguates what each consumer should extract.

sections.heading: ## Out of Scope
sections.include_when: Adjacent topics that authors might mistakenly add. Naming them explicitly prevents scope creep.

sections.heading: ## Related Knowledge
sections.include_when: Other knowledge files in knowledge/ that overlap or pair with this one. Avoids duplication and signals where to look for adjacent content.
sections.format: Per-file bullet list with one-line description

knowledge_json_skeleton._description: The top-level structure the skill writes to knowledge/<knowledge_name>.json. Shape-specific arrays (facts/principles/playbooks) are populated per knowledge_shapes.<shape>.json_required_fields. Placeholders use {{double_braces}}.

provenance.sources: {{array of source identifiers — non-empty when shape=reference}}

pre_run_checklist: Each entry in consumed_by[] resolves to an existing agent spec OR is being generated in the same session
post_run_checklist: knowledge/<knowledge_name>.md exists with all knowledge_md_required_sections present in order
knowledge/<knowledge_name>.json exists with shape set and the shape-specific required array present

changes: Initial knowledge file generated by make_agent_knowledge v1.0 (scaffold; body filled by author).

compact_boilerplate._description: Body skeletons per shape substituted into the generated knowledge MD. Each skeleton produces an empty-but-structured file — author fills the body in a follow-up edit. The runtime_strategy declaration is added to the JSON _metadata, not the MD.
compact_boilerplate.runtime_strategy_declaration_template: Set _metadata.runtime_strategy per runtime_strategy_rules below based on estimated token count of the filled-in body. Default for a freshly generated empty scaffold: 'embed' (will revisit when body is filled).

runtime_strategy_rules._description: Declarative thresholds for choosing _metadata.runtime_strategy. Token counts are estimated against the filled body, not the empty scaffold. The runner uses runtime_strategy to pick the host-platform primitive (see Cross-Platform Runtime Loading section in make_agent_knowledge.md).

rules.rationale: Below 2000 tokens, retrieval round-trip overhead exceeds the cost of inlining. Embed in the system message (or first user turn) and let prompt caching cover the repeated cost.

In the middle band, embed is preferred when the content is a stable cache-friendly prefix (set cache_control on Anthropic, rely on implicit caching on OpenAI/Google). Switch to read_at_runtime if the content changes frequently or if the runner needs first-class citation blocks (Anthropic Citations requires the document to be a content block; OpenAI file_search and xAI collections_search emit citations only via retrieval).

Past 8000 tokens, inlining burns too much context per call. Use the host platform's retrieval primitive: Anthropic Files API (file_id in document/image blocks, anthropic-beta: files-api-2025-04-14), OpenAI Responses API file_search over a vector store, Google Files API (files.upload, 48h retention) optionally combined with cachedContent, xAI collections_search.

runtime_strategy_rules.override_policy: Authors can override the auto-selected strategy by setting _metadata.runtime_strategy explicitly and adding a non-empty _metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale. KNW-QC-004 still enforces consistency between declared strategy and body size; a manual override must explain the deviation.

pre_run_checklist: knowledge/ folder exists at project root
shape input is one of reference | identity | procedural
knowledge_name is snake_case and has no existing .md or .json at knowledge/<knowledge_name>.{md,json} (or user has confirmed overwrite per P-002)
scope is non-empty and at least 20 characters
consumed_by[] has at least one entry
If shape=reference, provenance_sources is provided and non-empty
post_run_checklist: knowledge/<knowledge_name>.md exists with all knowledge_md_required_sections in order (title, scope, body, provenance, last_updated)
knowledge/<knowledge_name>.json exists with shape field set and the shape-specific required array key present (facts/principles/playbooks)
_metadata.runtime_strategy is set to one of embed | read_at_runtime | either, consistent with body size per runtime_strategy_rules
MD body has at least 100 bytes of content past the section headers (placeholder-only files rejected)
success_criteria: Generated knowledge file pair is shape-conformant per knowledge_shapes
BD-QC-001 through BD-QC-007 pass for this skill's own behavior

test_cases.name: Generate a reference-shape glossary
test_cases.input: shape=reference; knowledge_name=loan_underwriting_glossary; scope='Lender-specific underwriting term definitions'; consumed_by=['loan_classifier_agent.json']; provenance_sources=['Lender Risk Policy v4.2']
test_cases.expected_output: knowledge/loan_underwriting_glossary.md + .json created. JSON has shape=reference, facts=[], provenance.sources=['Lender Risk Policy v4.2'], _metadata.runtime_strategy set per runtime_strategy_rules. MD has title, scope, ## Terms heading, provenance, last_updated.

test_cases.name: Generate an identity-shape charter
test_cases.input: shape=identity; knowledge_name=loan_classifier_principles; scope='Working-style principles specific to loan_classifier_agent'; consumed_by=['loan_classifier_agent.json']
test_cases.expected_output: knowledge/loan_classifier_principles.md + .json created. JSON has shape=identity, principles=[], provenance.sources=[] permitted. MD has title, scope, ## The Principles heading, provenance (or self-source), last_updated.

test_cases.name: Generate a procedural-shape playbook set
test_cases.input: shape=procedural; knowledge_name=incident_response_playbooks; scope='Incident-response playbooks for on_call_agent'; consumed_by=['on_call_agent.json']
test_cases.expected_output: knowledge/incident_response_playbooks.md + .json created. JSON has shape=procedural, playbooks=[]. MD has title, scope, ## Playbooks heading, provenance, last_updated.

test_cases.name: Reject invalid shape value
test_cases.expected_output: Pre-run checklist fails on shape validation. P-001 — skill does not silently coerce. No files written.

test_cases.name: Reject knowledge file with empty body
test_cases.input: shape=identity; knowledge_name=empty_test; ... and the author submits a draft with body < 100 bytes
test_cases.expected_output: KNW-QC-001 fails — body content is below the 100-byte minimum. Skill explains: placeholder-only knowledge files are dead code. No files written.

test_cases.name: Reject silent overwrite of existing knowledge file
test_cases.expected_output: Per P-002, skill proposes diff against existing file, waits for confirmation. Does NOT silently overwrite.

qc_checks._description: Knowledge-file-specific QC checks. These are designed to merge into make_agent_qc.json as the KNW-QC family in a downstream task; they are NOT merged by this skill. Listed inline here for skill-internal reference and for the downstream merge.

checks.name: All required sections present in the generated MD
checks.rule: Generated knowledge MD contains all five sections from knowledge_md_required_sections in order: title, scope, body, provenance, last_updated. Plus the shape-specific extension section from knowledge_shapes.<shape>.md_extra_sections. The body must have at least 100 bytes of content past the section headers — placeholder-only files are rejected.

checks.name: shape field is a valid value
checks.rule: knowledge_json.shape is exactly one of 'reference', 'identity', or 'procedural'. No other values, no nulls. Invalid shapes break consumer parsing.

checks.name: Shape-specific required fields are populated
checks.rule: The shape-specific required JSON array key is present and the correct type: facts[] for shape=reference, principles[] for shape=identity, playbooks[] for shape=procedural. The array may be empty (placeholder), but the key MUST exist. Wrong-shape arrays (e.g., principles[] present when shape=reference) fail this check.

checks.name: runtime_strategy is set and consistent with body size
checks.rule: _metadata.runtime_strategy is one of 'embed' | 'read_at_runtime' | 'either'. The selected value is consistent with body token count per runtime_strategy_rules (< 2000 → embed, 2000-8000 → either, > 8000 → read_at_runtime), OR _metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale is non-empty and explains a deliberate override.

checks.name: provenance.sources is non-empty when shape=reference
checks.rule: When knowledge_json.shape == 'reference', provenance.sources[] MUST be non-empty. A reference file without sources cannot support citation traceback. Identity and procedural shapes are exempt (identity is self-sourcing; procedural may or may not reference external policy).

checks.name: consumed_by is non-empty
checks.rule: knowledge_json.consumed_by[] has at least one entry. A knowledge file that no agent consumes is dead code. Each entry should be a path to an agent spec (.json) that references this knowledge file via cross_references.knowledge_files[] — the cross-link itself is a downstream step, but the consumer must be named here.

checks.name: Reference shape + Anthropic structured output incompatibility
checks.rule: When knowledge_json.shape == 'reference' AND any agent in consumed_by[] declares an Anthropic model AND that agent's implementation.llm_agent.parameters.response_format (or output_config.format) is non-null, fail this check. Anthropic Citations and Structured Outputs cannot be combined (the API returns 400). Resolution options: (a) drop citations from the reference document, (b) drop structured output from the consuming agent, (c) split into two calls (citation pass + extraction pass). Severity high because this surfaces only at runtime, not at validation. Source: source_docs/anthropic_citations.md (incompatibility documented in the Warning callout).

resolved_decisions._description: Decisions resolved during the 2026-05-13 drafting cycle. Captured here so future readers know why these defaults are in place.
items: KNW-QC placement: rule_id 20 in make_agent_qc.json delegates to KNW-QC-001..006 here (same pattern as rule 17 delegating to BD-QC and rule 19 delegating to ORCH-QC). Single source of truth, not duplicated. Resolved 2026-05-13.

consumed_by[] validation: forward references are accepted at generation time. Rationale: a session may generate the knowledge file before the agent that will consume it, and tight validation would force unnatural ordering. KNW-QC-006 still fails on a file with NO consumers (knowledge with no consumer is dead code). Resolved 2026-05-13.

Token count estimation: rough chars/4 heuristic is the default for runtime_strategy_rules thresholds. Platforms differ in tokenization (BPE variants), so any threshold is approximate. A platform-specific tokenizer can be wired in at QC time per host if precision matters. Resolved 2026-05-13.

changes: Initial make_agent_knowledge skill — sibling to make_agent, make_AGENTS, and make_orchestrator_agent.
Three shapes supported: reference, identity, procedural — each with its own MD spine extension and required JSON array (facts/principles/playbooks).
Authoring scaffold only — emits empty-but-structured templates; no ingestion pipeline (per scoping decision 2026-05-12).
Always emits MD + JSON pair (per scoping decision 2026-05-12).
_metadata.runtime_strategy declared in the JSON per runtime_strategy_rules thresholds (< 2000 → embed / 2000-8000 → either / > 8000 → read_at_runtime).
6 knowledge-file QC checks (KNW-QC-001 through KNW-QC-006) embedded inline for downstream merge into make_agent_qc.json.

Cross-platform runtime loading documented for Anthropic (Files API + cache_control + Citations), OpenAI (Responses API file_search + vector stores), Google (Files API + cachedContent), xAI (collections_search). Grounded in source_docs/ refreshed 2026-05-12/13.
Frames identity-shape as the existing pattern this skill formalizes — exemplar is knowledge/behavioral_discipline.{md,json}.
